     Source: claude-plugin-harness-best-practices-en.md § 4.9.
     Last sync: 2026-04-18. -->
#### Gotchas — Check These Before Every Upload

**1. Reserved marketplace names**

```json
// ❌ Never use these
{ "name": "claude-code-marketplace" }
{ "name": "claude-plugins-official" }
{ "name": "anthropic-marketplace" }
```

Names starting with `anthropic`, `claude`, or `official` are reserved. An upload using these names will be rejected.

**2. version priority — silent override**

```json
// Even if marketplace.json declares this:
{ "plugins": [{ "name": "my-tool", "version": "2.0.0" }] }

// And plugin.json says this:
{ "version": "1.5.0" }

// Actual version applied: 1.5.0 (plugin.json always wins, silently)
```

When version is declared in both places, `plugin.json` always wins — without any warning. Don't assume the `marketplace.json` version takes effect.

**3. Relative paths only work with git-based Marketplaces**

```json
// ✅ Installed via git clone: relative path works
{ "source": "./plugins/my-tool" }

// ❌ Uploaded as a ZIP via URL: relative path is meaningless
//    → Use an absolute URL or github: format instead
{ "source": "./plugins/my-tool" }  // Does not work in URL-based contexts
```

**4. kebab-case is mandatory, not optional**

If a plugin `name` contains uppercase letters or underscores (`_`), local validate only warns — but claude.ai Marketplace sync will automatically reject it. Use kebab-case from the start.

```json
// ❌ Rejected
{ "name": "My_Plugin", "plugins": [{ "name": "MyTool" }] }

// ✅ Accepted
{ "name": "my-plugin", "plugins": [{ "name": "my-tool" }] }
```

---
